$metadata.description: Design token usage guidelines, extracted from Figma variable descriptions.

25.description:
Purpose: The lightest step in the teal primitive scale; a near-white tinted surface value.
Usage: Referenced by semantic tokens requiring a very subtle teal-tinted wash, such as colour/state/selected in light mode.
Guidelines: Do not apply directly to component design. All usage must flow through a semantic alias. Reserve for contexts where the tint is barely perceptible against a white background.

50.description:
Purpose: A soft, low-saturation teal primitive establishing the second lightest step in the scale.
Usage: Consumed by semantic tokens that require a light teal background, such as state and hover overlays.
Guidelines: Not for direct use in layouts or components. Should always be aliased through a semantic token.

100.description:
Purpose: A light teal primitive representing the third step of the scale.
Usage: Available for aliasing into semantic tokens where a light but visible teal fill is required.
Guidelines: Use only as a primitive source value. Do not hard-code into component styles.

200.description:
Purpose: A medium-light teal primitive at the fourth step of the scale.
Usage: Provides a mid-range light value for teal; usable as a source for subtle border or tinted fill tokens.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Must be consumed via a semantic token alias.

300.description:
Purpose: A mid-range teal primitive establishing a clearly visible, unsaturated teal.
Usage: Aliased into semantic tokens for interactive borders, icon fills, and link text in dark mode contexts.
Guidelines: Not for direct application. Check contrast ratio when aliasing to text or icon tokens — minimum 4.5:1 required for WCAG AA compliance.

400.description:
Purpose: A medium teal primitive at the fifth-brightest step of the scale.
Usage: Source value for semantic action, icon, and interactive element tokens in dark mode.
Guidelines: Validate contrast when used as foreground on dark surfaces. Not for direct use in component design.

500.description:
Purpose: The core brand teal; the primary mid-point of the scale and the anchor for the brand identity.
Usage: Aliased into colour/action/primary (light mode) and colour/background/brand. The single brand-colour source value provided by the brand designer.
Guidelines: Treat as the canonical brand colour. When aliasing to text or icon contexts, verify WCAG AA compliance. Do not substitute other primitives where teal/500 is expected.

600.description:
Purpose: A darkened brand teal primitive one step below the mid-point.
Usage: Aliased into hover state tokens for primary actions in light mode (colour/action/primary/hover).
Guidelines: Use to communicate interaction depth on primary actions. Maintain at least one step of contrast against the resting-state primitive.

700.description:
Purpose: A dark teal primitive providing strong contrast against light surfaces.
Usage: Aliased into pressed/active state tokens for primary actions in light mode, and into colour/background/promo.
Guidelines: Reserved for pressed states and promotional backgrounds. Do not use where a softer brand presence is required.

800.description:
Purpose: A very dark teal primitive approaching near-black saturation.
Usage: Available for aliasing into dark, high-contrast teal surfaces such as deep brand backgrounds.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Ensure any foreground aliased against this value meets 4.5:1 contrast for text.

900.description:
Purpose: The darkest teal near the bottom of the scale, suitable for deep inverse backgrounds.
Usage: Aliased into colour/background/inverse to provide the dark branded surface in light mode.
Guidelines: Only use in contexts where maximum teal depth is required. Foreground on this surface must be a near-white neutral.

950.description:
Purpose: The absolute darkest step of the teal scale; near-black with a teal undertone.
Usage: Available as a source value for the very deepest teal surfaces if required by future component needs.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Use sparingly — prefer teal/900 unless maximum depth is explicitly required.

0.description:
Purpose: The zero-point anchor of the neutral scale; pure white in light mode and the deepest dark-mode base in dark mode.
Usage: Aliased into colour/background/default and colour/background/raised in light mode; inverted to the darkest surface in dark mode.
Guidelines: A special-case primitive. Never substitute another step where neutral/0 is expected, as it carries structural meaning as both the lightest and darkest extremes depending on mode.

50.description:
Purpose: The lightest non-white step of the neutral scale; a very subtle off-white.
Usage: Aliased into colour/background/subtle and colour/background/surface to distinguish page-level tinted regions and component backgrounds from the default canvas.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Note that subtle and surface share this primitive intentionally — semantic differentiation is achieved through borders and elevation, not colour alone.

100.description:
Purpose: A light neutral primitive providing a soft, visible background distinction.
Usage: Aliased into colour/background/muted, colour/border/subtle, and colour/state/disabled/bg.
Guidelines: Not for direct use. When aliased to border tokens, ensure visual separation from adjacent surfaces is sufficient without relying on this colour alone.

200.description:
Purpose: A slightly darker light neutral, providing a clearly visible but low-contrast value.
Usage: Aliased into colour/border/default and colour/action/secondary for default borders and secondary action fills.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Border tokens aliasing this value must maintain a minimum 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent surfaces per WCAG AA.

300.description:
Purpose: A mid-light neutral sitting at a clearly visible step between light and mid tones.
Usage: Aliased into colour/border/strong and colour/action/secondary/hover.
Guidelines: Not for direct use. When aliased to interactive controls, verify that hover states provide sufficient visual change from the resting state.

400.description:
Purpose: A mid neutral at the boundary between light and medium tones.
Usage: Aliased into colour/text/tertiary (light mode), colour/state/disabled/text, and colour/action/secondary/pressed.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. When aliased to text tokens, ensure the resulting combination meets WCAG AA contrast against its background context.

500.description:
Purpose: The true mid-point of the neutral scale; a balanced grey with equal contrast in both directions.
Usage: Aliased into colour/text/secondary (light mode) and colour/onBackground/muted in light mode.
Guidelines: Not for direct use. The mirror relationship between neutral/500 and neutral/400 across modes is intentional — validate both mode outputs when modifying this token.

600.description:
Purpose: A mid-dark neutral providing stronger contrast than the mid-point.
Usage: Aliased into colour/text/secondary (light mode) and colour/onBackground/subtle in light mode.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Confirm WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 for body text) against all background contexts where this value is applied via semantic aliases.

700.description:
Purpose: A dark neutral close to the deep anchor, suitable for icon and secondary heading values.
Usage: Aliased into colour/icon/default to ensure icons read clearly against light surfaces.
Guidelines: Not for direct use. As an icon colour source, the semantic token should maintain 3:1 contrast against the surface it appears on.

800.description:
Purpose: A very dark neutral near the bottom of the light-mode scale.
Usage: Aliased into component-layer tokens and dark-mode background tokens requiring high-contrast dark fills.
Guidelines: Primitive-layer only. Use to support elevation distinctions in dark mode through subtle tonal differences.

900.description:
Purpose: The near-darkest neutral in the scale; a deep, ink-like tone for primary text and strong foreground elements.
Usage: Aliased into colour/text/primary and colour/onBackground/default in light mode.
Guidelines: Not for direct use. Always verify 7:1 contrast against the default background for AAA compliance, and minimum 4.5:1 for AA.

950.description:
Purpose: The darkest step in the neutral scale; the primary dark-mode base surface colour.
Usage: Aliased into colour/background/default in dark mode and colour/text/inverse in light mode.
Guidelines: A structural anchor primitive. Changes to this value propagate broadly — always audit all downstream semantic aliases before modifying.

default.description:
Purpose: The primary canvas background; the base surface of every page and layout.
Usage: Applied to the root body background, full-page layouts, and any area that represents the default visual plane of the UI.
Guidelines: Do not use for elevated or contained surfaces — use colour/background/surface or colour/background/raised instead.

info.description:
Purpose: The background fill for informational status surfaces, banners, and notification containers.
Usage: Applied to the container background of info alerts, info badges, and any UI surface communicating a neutral informational message.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/onBackground/info.

success.description:
Purpose: The background fill for success status surfaces, confirmations, and positive feedback containers.
Usage: Applied to success alert backgrounds, success badge fills, and any surface communicating a successfully completed action.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/onBackground/success.

error.description:
Purpose: The background fill for error status surfaces, failure messages, and critical feedback containers.
Usage: Applied to error alert backgrounds, error badge fills, and any surface communicating a failed action.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/onBackground/error.

warning.description:
Purpose: The background fill for warning status surfaces and cautionary feedback containers.
Usage: Applied to warning alert backgrounds, warning badge fills, and any surface communicating a cautionary condition.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/onBackground/warning. Amber tones are inherently lower in contrast — verify all text on this surface meets 4.5:1 WCAG AA.

default.description:
Purpose: The primary foreground colour for text and icons on the default background surface.
Usage: Applied to any text or icon element rendered directly on the default page canvas.
Guidelines: Use in combination with colour/background/default. Matches colour/text/primary in value — prefer colour/text/* for text-only contexts.

subtle.description:
Purpose: A secondary foreground colour for supporting text and icons on subtle or muted backgrounds.
Usage: Applied to supporting body copy, captions, and secondary icons on tinted or subtle page regions.
Guidelines: Verify WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 for text) against colour/background/subtle when applied.

muted.description:
Purpose: A tertiary foreground colour for reduced-emphasis content on any background.
Usage: Applied to placeholder text, metadata, disabled foreground labels, and any content intentionally subordinated in the visual hierarchy.
Guidelines: Do not use for interactive or actionable content.

inverse.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for content rendered on inverse (dark branded) surfaces.
Usage: Applied to all text and icons on colour/background/inverse to ensure legibility on dark teal surfaces.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/background/inverse.

brand.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for content on brand-coloured surfaces.
Usage: Applied to text and icons within brand-filled components, such as primary button labels and brand tile content.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/background/brand. Verify contrast at both light and dark mode values.

promo.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for content on promotional surfaces.
Usage: Applied to text and icons within deep teal promotional UI regions.
Guidelines: Always pair with colour/background/promo. Validate WCAG AA contrast.

info.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for text and icons rendered on info status surfaces.
Usage: Applied to the body text, headings, and inline icons within info alert containers and badges.
Guidelines: Only use on colour/background/info surfaces. Verify WCAG AA contrast in both modes.

success.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for text and icons on success status surfaces.
Usage: Applied to body text, headings, and icons within success alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Pair exclusively with colour/background/success.

error.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for text and icons on error status surfaces.
Usage: Applied to error message text, form validation labels, and icons within error alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Pair exclusively with colour/background/error.

warning.description:
Purpose: The foreground colour for text and icons on warning status surfaces.
Usage: Applied to warning message text and icons within warning alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Pair exclusively with colour/background/warning. Amber backgrounds make contrast compliance non-trivial — always validate 4.5:1.

subtle.description:
Purpose: The lightest border colour for minimal structural separation between adjacent surfaces.
Usage: Applied to card borders, divider lines, and container outlines where a light, unobtrusive boundary is sufficient.
Guidelines: Ensure a minimum 3:1 contrast against both adjacent surfaces for non-text UI components.

default.description:
Purpose: The standard border colour for most contained UI elements and form controls.
Usage: Applied to input fields, dropdown containers, modal outlines, and standard component borders in a resting state.
Guidelines: Use as the default form control border.

strong.description:
Purpose: A higher-contrast border for surfaces requiring emphatic separation or structural distinction.
Usage: Applied to strongly delineated sections, featured callout borders, and dividers requiring higher visual prominence.
Guidelines: Reserve for contexts where colour/border/default provides insufficient visual separation.

interactive.description:
Purpose: The brand-teal border colour signalling interactive focus and active selection states on form controls.
Usage: Applied to input field borders on :focus, selected control outlines, and any border communicating keyboard focus or active interaction.
Guidelines: Must always be applied to the focused state of all interactive inputs to satisfy WCAG 2.4.7.

info.description:
Purpose: The border colour for info status containers.
Usage: Applied to the border of info alert components and info-state input fields.
Guidelines: Maintain 3:1 contrast against both the info background and the adjacent page surface.

success.description:
Purpose: The border colour for success status containers.
Usage: Applied to the border of success alert components and success-state input fields.
Guidelines: Maintain 3:1 contrast against the success background and the surrounding page surface.

error.description:
Purpose: The border colour for error status containers and invalid form fields.
Usage: Applied to error alert borders and input field borders in an invalid or error state.
Guidelines: Maintain 3:1 contrast against the error background and adjacent surfaces.

warning.description:
Purpose: The border colour for warning status containers.
Usage: Applied to warning alert borders and any control border indicating a cautionary state.
Guidelines: Maintain 3:1 contrast against the warning background and surrounding page surface.

primary.description:
Purpose: The brand-teal fill for primary interactive action controls in their resting state.
Usage: Applied to primary button backgrounds and any control representing the most prominent call to action.
Guidelines: Use for the single most important action in a given context.

hover.description:
Purpose: The darkened brand-teal fill for primary action controls on cursor hover.
Usage: Applied exclusively to primary button and primary interactive control backgrounds on :hover.
Guidelines: The perceptible shift from the resting state confirms interactivity.

pressed.description:
Purpose: The deepest brand-teal fill for primary action controls in a pressed or active state.
Usage: Applied to primary button backgrounds on :active / pointer down.
Guidelines: Must be visually distinct from both the resting and hover states.

secondary.description:
Purpose: The neutral fill for secondary action controls in their resting state.
Usage: Applied to secondary button backgrounds and any control presenting an alternative or supplementary action.
Guidelines: Secondary actions should never visually compete with a co-located primary action.

hover.description:
Purpose: A slightly darker neutral fill for secondary action controls on cursor hover.
Usage: Applied to secondary button and secondary control backgrounds on :hover.
Guidelines: The hover shift should be clearly distinguishable from the resting state.

pressed.description:
Purpose: The deepest neutral fill for secondary action controls in a pressed state.
Usage: Applied to secondary button and secondary control backgrounds on :active.
Guidelines: Must be visually distinct from hover.

focus.description:
Purpose: The brand-teal colour used to render focus ring indicators on all interactive elements.
Usage: Applied to focus ring outlines on buttons, inputs, links, and any focusable control.
Guidelines: Must be applied to all keyboard-navigable interactive elements to satisfy WCAG 2.4.7.

hover.description:
Purpose: A subtle neutral overlay background applied on cursor hover to indicate that an element is interactive.
Usage: Applied to navigation items, list items, table rows, and any hover-sensitive container.
Guidelines: Use as a lightweight hover signal where a full action colour is too heavy.

selected.description:
Purpose: A soft teal-tinted background indicating a selected or chosen state.
Usage: Applied to selected navigation items, selected list rows, and active tab or filter chip backgrounds.
Guidelines: Pair with colour/text/primary for foreground content — the selected state colour alone should not convey selection without additional visual cues.

active.description:
Purpose: A slightly stronger teal tint than selected, used for actively engaged or pressed interactive list items.
Usage: Applied to the active (momentarily pressed) state of list items, nav entries, or any surface showing a transient active interaction.
Guidelines: Distinct from selected in visual weight — active is transient; selected is persistent.

text.description:
Purpose: The foreground text and label colour for disabled interactive components.
Usage: Applied to button labels, input text, and all text within disabled controls.
Guidelines: Pair with colour/state/disabled/bg. Disabled text is contrast-exempt under WCAG 1.4.3 but should remain perceptibly muted.

bg.description:
Purpose: The background fill for disabled interactive components; communicates unavailability.
Usage: Applied to the background of disabled buttons, disabled input fields, and any interactive control that is not currently operable.
Guidelines: Always accompany with colour/state/disabled/text.

default.description:
Purpose: The standard colour for standalone icons in neutral, non-interactive contexts.
Usage: Applied to decorative icons, informational icons, and supporting visual elements adjacent to body text.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against the background surface.

subtle.description:
Purpose: A reduced-emphasis colour for secondary or supporting icons.
Usage: Applied to auxiliary icons, trailing decorative glyphs, and icons used in low-priority informational contexts.
Guidelines: Reserve for visual support roles.

brand.description:
Purpose: The brand teal colour for icons requiring brand expression.
Usage: Applied to featured brand icons, primary action icons, and interactive icons in brand-forward contexts.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against the surface it appears on.

inverse.description:
Purpose: A light icon colour for use on dark or branded backgrounds.
Usage: Applied to icons on colour/background/inverse, dark panels, and any dark-filled surface requiring light foreground icons.
Guidelines: Pair only with surfaces dark enough to maintain 3:1 contrast.

info.description:
Purpose: The colour for icons within info status components.
Usage: Applied to the leading icon in info alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against colour/background/info.

success.description:
Purpose: The colour for icons within success status components.
Usage: Applied to the leading icon in success alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against colour/background/success.

error.description:
Purpose: The colour for icons within error status components.
Usage: Applied to the leading icon in error alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against colour/background/error.

warning.description:
Purpose: The colour for icons within warning status components.
Usage: Applied to the leading icon in warning alert and badge components.
Guidelines: Verify 3:1 contrast against colour/background/warning.

subtle.description: Decorative-only. Lightest divider for table row stripes and aesthetic hairlines. Exempt from 3:1 contrast — MUST NOT be used for functional boundaries.

default.description: Decorative-only. Standard divider for non-functional visual separations. Exempt from 3:1 contrast — use colour/border/default for functional boundaries.

strong.description: Decorative-only. Most visible divider for emphatic aesthetic separation. Exempt from 3:1 contrast — use colour/border/strong for functional boundaries.

onBrand.description: Button-safe text for brand surfaces. MUST NOT alias colour/text/*. White in both modes. Verify 4.5:1 vs all action.primary states.

onNeutral.description: Button-safe text for neutral surfaces. MUST NOT alias colour/text/*. Verify 4.5:1 vs all action.secondary states in both modes.

bg.description:
Purpose: The background fill of text input fields in their default resting state.
Usage: Applied to the background of all text inputs, textareas, and select components.
Guidelines: References colour/background/surface.

text.description:
Purpose: The text colour for user-entered content within input fields.
Usage: Applied to the value text of all text inputs and textareas.
Guidelines: References colour/text/primary. Verify 4.5:1 contrast against input/bg.

placeholder.description:
Purpose: The text colour for placeholder content in input fields before user entry.
Usage: Applied to ::placeholder pseudo-element styling on all text inputs and textareas.
Guidelines: Placeholder text is not a substitute for a visible label.

border.description:
Purpose: The default border colour for input fields in a resting, unfocused state.
Usage: Applied to the border of all inputs, textareas, and select components when not in a special state.
Guidelines: This is the base state — always apply focus, error, and success border tokens as state overrides.

focus.description:
Purpose: The border colour for input fields when focused by keyboard or pointer.
Usage: Applied to the input border on :focus and :focus-visible states.
Guidelines: Required for WCAG 2.4.7 compliance.

error.description:
Purpose: The border colour indicating an invalid or error state on an input field.
Usage: Applied to the border of inputs that have failed validation.
Guidelines: Always accompany with an inline error message.

success.description:
Purpose: The border colour indicating a valid or successfully validated input field.
Usage: Applied to the border of inputs that have passed validation.

bg.description:
Purpose: The background fill of the primary button in its default resting state.
Usage: Applied to the background of all primary button instances across the UI.
Guidelines: References colour/action/primary — do not override with a raw primitive.

Purpose: The background fill of the secondary button in its default resting state.
Usage: Applied to the background of all secondary button instances.
Guidelines: Secondary buttons should co-exist alongside primary actions without competing.

Purpose: The background fill of the navigation bar container.
Usage: Applied to the root background of the utility bar, main navigation bar, and any top-level navigation container.

Purpose: The background fill of card components.
Usage: Applied to the background of all card variants — content cards, media cards, data summary cards.

base.description: Primary typeface for all UI text. Inter Tight provides a modern, condensed aesthetic optimised for digital interfaces.

annotation.description: Monospace typeface for code snippets, technical labels, and annotation content.

display.description: Largest text scale. Used for hero headings and major marketing moments. Not for body copy or UI components.

headline.description: Section and page-level headings. Used for H1–H3 equivalents within product UI.

title.description: Component-level headings, card titles, and subheadings within content sections.

body.description: Default reading text. Used for all paragraph and descriptive content.

label.description: Smallest text scale. Used for badges, tags, form labels, and inline metadata.
